assertions.title: Image Generation Returns Typed Payloads and Streamed Image Events

steps.action: Run the assigned live image smoke from the repo root with creds loaded in-shell and OPENAI_BASE_URL unset
steps.expected: The budget-capped image smoke should hit the default OpenAI host, stream at least one partial image event, finish with a completed event carrying non-empty base64 image data, and expose a non-empty request ID
steps.observed: The test passed. Output reported request ID `req_f731b2069026497c895a763c90e51ae8`, `partial image events observed: 1`, and `completed image bytes (base64 chars): 2094848`.

evidence.network: Live OpenAI API image generation stream succeeded via the default host path with one streamed partial image event followed by terminal completion.
evidence.defaultHostProof: The command explicitly unset OPENAI_BASE_URL before the smoke. No explicit base-url line was printed by the test output.

assertions.reason: The assigned live smoke passed and printed a real request ID plus streamed partial/completed image evidence, satisfying the live-smoke portion of VAL-MEDIA-001 within the single-image budget cap.

assertions.title: Audio Transcription Dispatches Output Types from Multipart Uploads

steps.action: Run the assigned live audio transcription smoke from the repo root with creds loaded in-shell and OPENAI_BASE_URL unset
steps.expected: The budget-capped transcription smoke should use the default host, generate a tiny WAV fixture through the SDK speech surface, successfully transcribe it through the SDK transcription surface, return non-empty text, and expose a non-empty request ID
steps.observed: The test passed. Output reported transcription text `Hello from OpenAI Rust!` and request ID `req_efd33d83a43a41d6a1de569ed4b04003`.

steps.action: Inspect the assigned live transcription smoke source for default-host proof
steps.expected: The live test should prove default-host resolution when OPENAI_BASE_URL is unset
steps.observed: The test source resolves client config and asserts `resolved.base_url == DEFAULT_BASE_URL`; the command also unsets OPENAI_BASE_URL before execution. The test output itself did not print the host.

evidence.network: Live OpenAI API speech generation and multipart transcription both succeeded during the smoke run.
evidence.defaultHostProof: Source assertion in tests/live_audio_transcription_smoke.rs confirms `resolved.base_url` equals `DEFAULT_BASE_URL` when OPENAI_BASE_URL is unset.

assertions.reason: The assigned live transcription smoke passed, produced non-empty transcription text, exposed a real request ID, and the test source plus unset environment prove the default-host path required by the live-smoke portion of VAL-MEDIA-004.

commands.command: set -a && . ./creds.txt && set +a && unset OPENAI_BASE_URL && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/validation/media/user-testing/target/live_media cargo test --test live_images_smoke -- --ignored --nocapture
commands.observation: Passed `live_image_stream_smoke_captures_request_id_and_completed_event`; output showed request ID `req_f731b2069026497c895a763c90e51ae8`, 1 partial image event, and 2,094,848 base64 characters in the completed image payload.

commands.command: set -a && . ./creds.txt && set +a && unset OPENAI_BASE_URL && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/validation/media/user-testing/target/live_media cargo test --test live_audio_transcription_smoke -- --ignored --nocapture
commands.observation: Passed `live_audio_transcription_smoke_captures_request_id`; output showed transcription text `Hello from OpenAI Rust!` and request ID `req_efd33d83a43a41d6a1de569ed4b04003`.

frictions.description: The live tests print request IDs and result summaries, but they do not print an explicit resolved base URL line.
frictions.resolution: Captured the required command environment (`unset OPENAI_BASE_URL`) and added a source-based proof note showing the live transcription smoke asserts `resolved.base_url == DEFAULT_BASE_URL`.

summary: Ran both assigned budget-capped live media smokes serially against the real OpenAI API. VAL-MEDIA-001 and VAL-MEDIA-004 both passed, with real request IDs captured for each run and supplemental default-host proof recorded for the transcription smoke.
